mission: Deep public-record Epstein file watch. Track official drops, court records, document releases, archive uploads, docket movement, exhibit references, deposition references, flight-log references, institutional records, financial links, and credible reporting. Separate proof, allegation, association, commentary, and unsupported viral claims.

topFigure.label: Child exploitation signal baseline
topFigure.subfigure: 32,167 missing-child cases supported · 1.4M online enticement reports · NCMEC 2025
topFigure.note: This is not an Epstein-specific victim count and not a global trafficking total. It is a public-source signal showing the scale of reported child exploitation material and online exploitation indicators.

watchLanes: Archive uploads, PDFs, exhibits, indexes, and metadata
Flight logs, travel references, calendar records, address-book references
Victim litigation, sworn claims, and institutional failure records
Financial, banking, philanthropic, academic, media, and reputation-protection links
Intelligence-adjacent claims, clearly separated from confirmed record
Symbolic, occult, ritual, or dark-culture claims labelled as commentary unless evidenced

bulletins.label: Epstein File Watch
bulletins.headline: New Mexico Zorro Ranch probe opens prosecutor-record lane
bulletins.summary: New Mexico's Epstein inquiry has been reported as seeking records connected to prosecutorial decisions and Epstein-related investigative history. This is treated as a public-record watch lane until specific documents are released and reviewed.
bulletins.why: The important question is not only who visited Epstein properties, but which institutions had records, which agencies acted, which did not, and what documentary trail exists.
bulletins.sourceLabel: Official commission / reputable reporting to be attached as released

bulletins.label: Epstein File Watch
bulletins.headline: Epstein preservation lane expands toward banks, tech, travel, and communications records
bulletins.summary: The Command Center tracks preservation demands and record requests involving banking, technology, travel, communications, property, employee, legal, and institutional records connected to Epstein-related matters.
bulletins.why: The deeper record is often not a headline; it is the metadata trail: wires, travel logs, correspondence, calendars, address books, visitor logs, legal memos, and institutional preservation letters.
bulletins.sourceLabel: Court / commission / official letters / reputable reporting

bulletins.label: Epstein File Watch
bulletins.headline: Academic institution records remain a key Epstein file lane
bulletins.summary: The Command Center tracks university records, donation histories, correspondence, advisory roles, introductions, institutional access, post-conviction relationships, and reputation-management patterns connected to Epstein.
bulletins.why: Elite access is often laundered through philanthropy, academic prestige, donor networks, introductions, and institutional silence. This lane separates documented access from unsupported guilt claims.
bulletins.sourceLabel: Congressional requests / institutional records / reputable reporting
